personalContext.summary: User is a 10-year experienced badminton doubles player who favors a tactical 'clever touch' (巧劲) play style over brute force. They prefer casual, humorous communication and enjoy lighthearted jokes during interactions.

topOfMind.summary: 1. Recently completed a weekend badminton match using 27lb string tension on his Yonex Astrox 100ZZ racket, currently focusing on solidifying optimized swing mechanics: extending backswing to leverage 蹬转 (drive turn) force to drive racket movement, avoiding arm-only发力 to protect his recovered shoulder. 2. Refining a suite of doubles tactical techniques: realistic fake kill drop shots, maintaining low center of gravity for faster starts, optimized net play (upright elbow for short controlled pushes, prioritizing cuts and light taps over frequent deep backcourt lifts to avoid being countered). 3. Continuing to follow proven post-training recovery routines: foam rolling for glute and upper back soreness, targeted stretching, and prioritizing pre-match warmups for injury-prone areas (shoulders, ankles, glutes) before play, controlling match intensity to ~70% to avoid re-injury. 4. Has finalized Victor 9200 as his preferred badminton shoe model (preferred over Li-Ning for better grip, sufficient cushioning for his light/medium weight) with a 500 RMB budget, currently evaluating color options for purchase.

recentMonths.summary: Over the past 2 months, the user has recovered from two acute badminton-related injuries: a shoulder strain and right ankle sprain, following RICE recovery protocols including icing and controlled rest periods. After a 2-week break for ankle recovery, he successfully returned to training, first mastering then fully consolidating the 'light step slow return' (轻踩慢回) footwork start-up technique which reduces tripping risk, as well as early racket frame preparation (架拍) for net and backhand shot stability, integrating both skills fully into regular training. He has upgraded his equipment: purchased a highly satisfying Yonex Astrox 100ZZ racket, adjusted string tension from his usual 28lb to 27lb to protect his recently recovered shoulder, and confirmed Victor 9200 as his preferred badminton shoe (better grip than Li-Ning, sufficient cushioning for his light/medium weight) with a 500 RMB budget, currently evaluating color options. He has recently refined multiple high-value doubles tactical insights: extending backswing to solidify蹬转-driven swing mechanics, deceptive fake kill drop shot technique, low center of gravity for faster reaction, and optimized net play tactics to avoid giving opponents offensive opportunities. He follows consistent post-training recovery routines including pre-training glute activation, post-training stretching and foam rolling, which effectively reduce soreness in shoulders, wrists and glutes, and adheres to safety protocols including full pre-match warmups for injury-prone areas and controlling match intensity to 70% to avoid re-injury.

longTermBackground.summary: User is an experienced badminton player with 10 years of play history, who regularly participates in doubles matches and values technical precision and tactical awareness. They have a longstanding preference for a 'clever touch' (巧劲) play style over brute force, prioritizing consistency and smart tactical choices over maximum power output during matches.
